steps.title: Find the axis (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly sketch a tall vertical line — this is the axis of the bottle. Everything you draw should mirror evenly around it, so it's worth getting straight.

steps.title: Block the bottle (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch the silhouette: a narrow neck at the top, a gentle shoulder where it widens, and straight sides down to the base. Keep both sides equal distance from your axis line.

steps.title: Mark the rim and base (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, add a short shallow ellipse at the top opening and another at the base. The base ellipse is a hint that the bottle is sitting on a surface.

steps.title: Plan the flower (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, lightly sketch a single stem rising up out of the bottle's mouth, leaning gently to one side. Mark a small circle at the top where the bloom will sit.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the bottle
steps.description: Switch to ink. Trace the bottle's silhouette in confident strokes — both side walls, the shoulder curve, and the rim ellipse. Stop the back of the rim where the stem will cover it.

steps.title: Ink the flower (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, draw the stem rising from the bottle's mouth and a small bloom at the top — five or six short curved petals around your circle guide. Add one small leaf partway down the stem.

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and remove the axis line and any pencil guides that distract. A few faint traces on the glass body are fine — they can read as reflections.
